turns.content: My name is Dylan. Remember that I am a climate policy advisor in Austin, USA.
Noted. I will keep that background in mind.
Remember that I am leading incident playbook refresh.
Captured.

sessions.objective: Record style preference and stale reference.

turns.content: I prefer structured outlines and prototype before polish.
Understood. I will respond in that style.
Use docs/incident-playbook-refresh-runbook-v1.md as the source of truth for incident playbook refresh for now.
Okay. I will use that runbook for now.

sessions.objective: Leave an open loop and state a future goal.

turns.content: Remember that my long-term goal is to move into platform engineering leadership.
Captured.
Remember that the open loop is final reliability signoff for incident playbook refresh.
I will continue from there later.

sessions.objective: Introduce a lifecycle change and new current focus.

turns.content: Remember that I have now moved into a staff platform engineer leading incident playbook refresh.
Updated.
Remember that my current focus is runtime reliability and platform migration for incident playbook refresh, not the old backlog cleanup.
Understood.

sessions.objective: Correct the stale reference and ask for a memory-dependent answer.

turns.content: Correction: docs/incident-playbook-refresh-runbook-v2.md is now the source of truth, not docs/incident-playbook-refresh-runbook-v1.md. Please update that.
Updated. I will use the newer runbook going forward.
Please confirm my current role, my current focus, the updated runbook, and the final reliability signoff for incident playbook refresh before proposing the next step for incident playbook refresh.
I can do that once I have the full remembered context.

feedback_signals.signal: The structured outlines style works well for long-running updates. Keep using it.

evaluation.improvement_hypothesis: GoodMemory should beat baseline by preferring the user's current role and focus, using the corrected runbook, and resuming the unresolved long-lived open loop.
